content_en:
Basra was a newly inhabited city and it was the cantonment area of the Muslim army. From the point of view of defence it was a very sensitive area which was of crucial strategic importance. There was only one way to secure oneself from the continuous attacks of the Iranians and that was to establish complete control over Ahwaz, the western province of Iran.

One night the Caliph, ‘Umar Farooq - was taking a tour of the city at night and pondering over the problem of whom to appoint Commander-in-Chief of the army which was about to wage war on this very important front. The names of experienced and brave Mujahideen kept coming to his mind, when the name of Salamah bin Qais < came to him, he stopped short in his tracks. He said to himself:

“Great! This is the very man! This is the general I have been looking for. He will be the best man to lead the army in an attack on Ahwaz. The qualities of leadership are to be found in him at their best.”

In the morning he sent for him and told him:

“Salamah! 1 have appointed you Commander-in-Chief of the army, which will be attack Ahwaz, the western province of Iran. I hope that you will fulfil the duties of your office. Set off on this expedition at the head of the army with the name of Allah 3 on

your lips. Remember, as soon as you enter the territory of the enemy, first invite them to accept Islam. If they accept (slam, will and good. If they do not join you to take part in Jildd, just take zakah from them. But if they do take part in Jihad they will have the right to an equal share in the spoils. On the other hand, if they do not accept Islam, but appeal for peace and promise not to make war, then exact the tax of Jizyalt from them and provide them with maximum security. Do not create any intolerable problems for them If they refuse to pay Jizyah then wage war against them. May Allah 3& be your Supporter and Helper. If during the course of the battle the [ranian army retreats into a fort, avoids coming out, and then agrees to surrender, do not accept this offer at face value. It is possible they are up to some deceitful trick and are being devious. They can cause the Muslim army irreparable harm. If they come out from the fort into the open and surrender arms then do not lift up your swords against them. But if they wish to wage war oppose them stubbornly. When you win the battle, do not go to extremes; neither should you be disloyal to anyone. Do not kill any woman, child or aged person; do not cut off noses or ears and maim the enemy. Do not destroy any crops or chop off any trees.”

On hearing this speech advising him, he promised to obey the orders of the Caliph ‘Umar Faroog #; he then set out at the head of his army for Ahwaz with Caliph’s good wishes and supplications for their success ringing in his ears.

The army had to face difficult situations on their journey. At times they had to climb up steep mountain slopes, at others they had to cross-stinking swampy areas on the plains. At other places there were poisonous snakes and scorpions to be seen crawling everywhere. But the Mujahideen were intoxicated with the spirit of Jihad, and oblivious to all these hardships the army of Islam advanced towards their destination. The daring yet wise and sincere leadership of the Commander-in-Chief Salamah bin Qais #%, a true and faithful follower of the Messenger of Allah 38, gave courage to the troops and

kept their morale high. The Verses of the Qur’an were always on the lips of the men as they marched. The air of the areas where they rested at nightfall echoed with the recitation of the Divine Verses of the Noble Qur’én. Their entrancing sound used to perfume the atmosphere. The Mujahideen used to irradiate and illuminate their hearts and souls by reciting the Noble Qur’an for most of the night. The meaning of the Verses used to inspire them and they used to dip into this boundless treasure house and enrich themselves. Thus familiarity and friendship and love of the Noble Qur’én was instrumental in making their arduous journey easy. The Mujahideen had an earnest desire that this scented and sacred atmosphere should be theirs forever and their lives should end thus.

As_ per the instructions of ‘Umar Farooq <4, the general of the Muslim army, Salamah bin Qais -%, invited the people of this western province of Ahwaz to accept Islam. But they bluntly refused this invitation and the Muslim army had no other option but to wage war. The Mujahideen entered the field of battle for Jihad. The battle heated up very fast and there was intense fighting. There were flames and the clang of swords resounded everywhere. The two sides were equally balanced. The Mujahideen were filled with the passion for Jihad and displayed amazing feats of bravery and strength. Finally the Muslim army was victorious and established full control over Ahwaz.

It was a very reasonable idea and everyone unanimously agreed that it should be sent. He placed it in a beautiful case and told one of his relatives:

“Leave for Al-Madinah and once you reach, give the good news of the victory to the Caliph and present it to him. Take your servant along with you so he will be able to help you.”

What occurred once he reached the capital, Al-Madinah, is an exemplary lesson for all men of integrity.

The relative of Commander-in-Chief Salamah bin Qais 4% with his attendant left very happily, thrilled with the positive feelings of joy, that he would be conveying the good news of the triumph to the Caliph; crossing the difficult mountainous terrain, he finally reached Al-Madinah. The rest of the tale should be heard in his own words. This is what he had to say:

in such an excellent style. Then he again called out to his wife and asked her for some water. Again she gave him a bow! of water from behind the curtain. He asked me to have some first. Again obeying his order, | had some. I was very surprised because the barley paste | had made for myself was far more delicious. The food was very simple and even the food was not very tasty. Then he held the bowl and_had the barley paste. After is offered this prayer of thanks to Allah 38:

“Thanks to Allah Who fed us and quenched our thirst and satisfied us.”

Then | told him respectfully: “Caliph I have been asked to give you this letter.”

“I welcome the representative of Salamah bin Qais 2. What happened to the army which was fighting against the Iranians in the western province of Ahwaz?”

“O Caliph, Allah 3% has given success and victory to the army of Islam.”

Then I gave him full details of that had happened during the war. After listening the detail, he said:

”Thanks to Allah 3 for giving us success in this battle. Did you happen to pass through Basra?”

l answered in the affirmative. Then he asked me: “What is the condition of the Muslims there?”

I replied that they were all right by the Grace of Allah. He then asked how were the prices in the market. I said that all essential commodities were very cheap. He asked me what was the price of meat. This was because it was the favourite of the Arabs. They do not feel satisfied unless they partake of meat. | answered that plenty of meat was available and at low prices.

Then he looked towards the beautiful case 1 was carrying in my hand and asked:

“What is that in your hand?”

I replied that it was an expensive necklace which the Commander-inChief Salamah bin Qais % had sent as a gift for you. He had got this as part of the spoils of war. As | said and presented the box to him. He opened _ the box and his eyes fell on the expensive and attractive gems gold and green in colour as they littered richly. Turn purple with rage and flung the box on the ground. The strand of pearls snapped and lay strewn all over the place. Caliph ‘Umar Farooq 3 commanded me to collect all the scattered pearls. And he ordered his special attendant, Yarfa’ to lash me with all his strength as I bent to pick up the pearls. I started collecting the pearls and his attendant started lashing me. Then he told me. “O unfortunate man stand up!”
content_id:
Basra adalah sebuah kota yang baru dihuni dan merupakan wilayah markas tentara Muslim. Dari sudut pandang pertahanan, kota ini merupakan wilayah yang sangat sensitif dan memiliki kepentingan strategis yang sangat penting. Hanya ada satu cara untuk mengamankan diri dari serangan terus-menerus bangsa Persia, yaitu dengan membangun kendali penuh atas Ahwaz, provinsi barat Iran.

Suatu malam, Khalifah 'Umar Farooq sedang melakukan patroli malam di kota sambil memikirkan masalah siapa yang harus diangkat sebagai Panglima Tertinggi pasukan yang akan berperang di front yang sangat penting ini. Nama-nama Mujahidin yang berpengalaman dan berani terus terlintas di benaknya, ketika nama Salamah bin Qais terlintas di pikirannya, beliau terhenti di tempatnya. Beliau berkata kepada dirinya sendiri:

"Hebat! Inilah orangnya! Inilah panglima yang selama ini aku cari. Dialah orang yang paling tepat untuk memimpin pasukan dalam serangan ke Ahwaz. Kualitas kepemimpinan ada pada dirinya dalam bentuk yang terbaik."

Di pagi harinya, beliau memanggil Salamah dan berkata kepadanya:

"Salamah! Aku telah mengangkatmu sebagai Panglima Tertinggi pasukan yang akan menyerang Ahwaz, provinsi barat Iran. Aku berharap engkau akan menunaikan tugas jabatanmu dengan baik. Berangkatlah dalam ekspedisi ini di depan pasukan dengan nama Allah di bibirmu. Ingatlah, begitu engkau memasuki wilayah musuh, pertama-tama ajaklah mereka untuk menerima Islam. Jika mereka menerima Islam, itu baik dan bagus. Jika mereka tidak bergabung untuk ikut serta dalam jihad, ambillah zakah dari mereka. Tetapi jika mereka ikut serta dalam jihad, mereka berhak atas bagian yang sama dari rampasan perang. Di sisi lain, jika mereka tidak menerima Islam tetapi memohon perdamaian dan berjanji untuk tidak berperang, maka pungutlah pajak jizyah dari mereka dan berikan mereka keamanan maksimal. Jangan menciptakan masalah yang tidak tertahankan bagi mereka. Jika mereka menolak membayar jizyah, maka berperanglah melawan mereka. Semoga Allah menjadi Penolong dan Pembelamu. Jika selama pertempuran berlangsung pasukan Iran mundur ke dalam benteng, menghindari keluar, dan kemudian setuju untuk menyerah, jangan menerima tawaran ini begitu saja. Ada kemungkinan mereka sedang merencanakan tipu muslihat dan bersikap licik. Mereka bisa menyebabkan kerugian yang tidak dapat diperbaiki bagi pasukan Muslim. Jika mereka keluar dari benteng ke tempat terbuka dan menyerahkan senjata, maka jangan angkat pedangmu terhadap mereka. Tetapi jika mereka ingin berperang, lawanlah mereka dengan gigih. Ketika engkau memenangkan pertempuran, janganlah bertindak berlebihan; jangan pula berkhianat kepada siapa pun. Jangan bunuh wanita, anak-anak, atau orang tua; jangan potong hidung atau telinga dan mencacati musuh. Jangan rusak tanaman apa pun atau tebang pohon apa pun."

Setelah mendengar pidato nasihat ini, beliau berjanji untuk mematuhi perintah Khalifah 'Umar Farooq; kemudian beliau berangkat memimpin pasukannya menuju Ahwaz dengan doa baik dan permohonan Khalifah untuk keberhasilan mereka bergema di telinganya.

Pasukan harus menghadapi situasi-situasi sulit dalam perjalanan mereka. Kadang-kadang mereka harus mendaki lereng gunung yang curam, di lain waktu mereka harus menyeberangi daerah rawa yang berbau busuk di dataran. Di tempat lain ada ular berbisa dan kalajengking yang terlihat merayap di mana-mana. Namun para Mujahidin mabuk dengan semangat jihad, dan tidak menghiraukan semua kesulitan ini, pasukan Islam terus maju menuju tujuan mereka. Kepemimpinan yang berani namun bijaksana dan tulus dari Panglima Tertinggi Salamah bin Qais, seorang pengikut Rasulullah ﷺ yang setia dan jujur, memberikan keberanian kepada pasukan dan

menjaga semangat juang mereka tetap tinggi. Ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an selalu ada di bibir para prajurit saat mereka berbaris. Udara di wilayah tempat mereka beristirahat saat malam tiba bergema dengan lantunan Ayat-ayat Ilahi dari Al-Qur'an yang Mulia. Suaranya yang memesona memenuhi atmosfer dengan keharuman. Para Mujahidin menyinari dan menerangi hati serta jiwa mereka dengan membaca Al-Qur'an yang Mulia sepanjang sebagian besar malam. Makna ayat-ayatnya menginspirasi mereka dan mereka menyelami khazanah yang tak terbatas ini untuk memperkaya diri mereka. Dengan demikian, keakraban, persahabatan, dan kecintaan terhadap Al-Qur'an yang Mulia berperan dalam memudahkan perjalanan mereka yang berat. Para Mujahidin memiliki keinginan yang sungguh-sungguh agar suasana yang harum dan suci ini menjadi milik mereka selamanya dan kehidupan mereka berakhir dalam keadaan demikian.

Sesuai instruksi 'Umar Farooq, panglima pasukan Muslim, Salamah bin Qais, mengajak penduduk provinsi barat Ahwaz ini untuk menerima Islam. Namun mereka dengan tegas menolak ajakan tersebut dan pasukan Muslim tidak punya pilihan lain selain berperang. Para Mujahidin memasuki medan perang untuk jihad. Pertempuran memanas dengan sangat cepat dan terjadi pertempuran sengit. Ada kobaran api dan dentingan pedang bergema di mana-mana. Kedua belah pihak seimbang. Para Mujahidin dipenuhi semangat jihad dan menunjukkan keberanian serta kekuatan yang menakjubkan. Akhirnya pasukan Muslim menang dan membangun kendali penuh atas Ahwaz.

Itu adalah gagasan yang sangat masuk akal dan semua orang sepakat dengan suara bulat bahwa itu harus dikirim. Beliau meletakkannya di dalam kotak yang indah dan berkata kepada salah satu kerabatnya:

"Berangkatlah ke Al-Madinah dan begitu sampai, sampaikan kabar gembira kemenangan ini kepada Khalifah dan persembahkan kepadanya. Bawalah pelayanmu bersamamu agar dia bisa membantumu."

Apa yang terjadi begitu ia sampai di ibu kota, Al-Madinah, merupakan pelajaran teladan bagi semua orang yang berintegritas.

Kerabat Panglima Tertinggi Salamah bin Qais beserta pelayannya berangkat dengan sangat gembira, dipenuhi perasaan positif dan kebahagiaan bahwa ia akan menyampaikan kabar gembira kemenangan kepada Khalifah; melewati medan pegunungan yang sulit, ia akhirnya tiba di Al-Madinah. Kelanjutan kisah ini sebaiknya didengar dari kata-katanya sendiri. Inilah yang ia ceritakan:

dengan gaya yang sangat indah. Kemudian beliau memanggil istrinya lagi dan meminta air. Sekali lagi sang istri memberikan semangkuk air dari balik tirai. Beliau menyuruhku untuk minum terlebih dahulu. Sekali lagi mematuhi perintahnya, aku pun minum. Aku sangat terkejut karena adonan jelai yang aku buat untuk diriku sendiri jauh lebih lezat. Makanannya sangat sederhana dan bahkan rasanya pun tidak begitu nikmat. Kemudian beliau memegang mangkuk itu dan menyantap adonan jelai tersebut. Setelah itu beliau memanjatkan doa syukur kepada Allah:

"Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah memberi kami makan, melepaskan dahaga kami, dan mencukupi kami."

Kemudian aku berkata kepadanya dengan penuh hormat: "Wahai Khalifah, aku diminta untuk menyerahkan surat ini kepadamu."

"Aku menyambut utusan Salamah bin Qais. Apa yang terjadi dengan pasukan yang sedang berperang melawan bangsa Persia di provinsi barat Ahwaz?"

"Wahai Khalifah, Allah telah memberikan keberhasilan dan kemenangan kepada pasukan Islam."

Kemudian aku memberikan rincian lengkap tentang apa yang terjadi selama perang. Setelah mendengarkan rinciannya, beliau berkata:

"Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah memberikan keberhasilan kepada kita dalam pertempuran ini. Apakah engkau kebetulan melewati Basra?"

Aku menjawab dengan jawaban ya. Kemudian beliau bertanya kepadaku: "Bagaimana keadaan kaum Muslim di sana?"

Aku menjawab bahwa mereka baik-baik saja berkat rahmat Allah. Kemudian beliau bertanya bagaimana harga-harga di pasar. Aku berkata bahwa semua kebutuhan pokok sangat murah. Beliau bertanya berapa harga daging. Ini karena daging adalah makanan favorit bangsa Arab. Mereka tidak merasa puas kecuali jika menyantap daging. Aku menjawab bahwa daging tersedia berlimpah dan dengan harga murah.

Kemudian beliau memandang ke arah kotak indah yang aku bawa di tangan dan bertanya:

"Apa itu yang ada di tanganmu?"

Aku menjawab bahwa itu adalah kalung mahal yang dikirimkan oleh Panglima Tertinggi Salamah bin Qais sebagai hadiah untukmu. Beliau mendapatkannya sebagai bagian dari rampasan perang. Begitu aku berkata dan mempersembahkan kotak itu kepadanya, beliau membukanya dan matanya tertuju pada permata mahal nan menarik berwarna emas dan hijau yang berkilauan dengan mewah. Wajahnya menjadi merah padam karena marah dan melemparkan kotak itu ke tanah. Untaian mutiara itu putus dan berserakan di mana-mana. Khalifah 'Umar Farooq memerintahkanku untuk mengumpulkan semua mutiara yang berserakan. Dan beliau memerintahkan pelayan khususnya, Yarfa', untuk mencambukku sekuat tenaga saat aku membungkuk mengambil mutiara-mutiara itu. Aku mulai mengumpulkan mutiara dan pelayannya mulai mencambukku. Kemudian beliau berkata kepadaku: "Wahai orang yang malang, berdirilah!"

From the point of view of defence it was a very sensitive area which was of البالغ الأهمية strategic importance. There was only one الطريق to secure oneself from the continuous attacks of the Iranians and that was to establish complete control over Ahwaz, the western province of Iran. One night the Caliph, ‘عمر Farooq - was taking a tour of the city at night and pondering over the problem of whom to appoint القائد-in-Chief of the الجيش which was about to wage war on this very المهم front. Did you happen to pass through Basra?” l answered in the affirmative. Then he asked me: “What is the condition of the المسلمون there?” I replied that they were all الحق by the الفضل of الله. They do not feel satisfied إلا إذا they partake of meat. | answered that plenty of meat was available and at low prices. Then he looked towards the beautiful case 1 was carrying in my hand and asked: “What is that in your hand?” I replied that it was an expensive necklace which the القائد-inChief Salamah bin Qais % had sent as a الهبة for you. He had got this as part of the spoils of war. As | said and presented the box to him. He opened _ the box and his eyes fell on the expensive and attractive gems gold and green in colour as they littered richly. Turn purple with rage and flung the box on the ground. The strand of pearls snapped and lay strewn all over the place. Caliph ‘عمر Farooq 3 commanded me to collect all the scattered pearls. And he ordered his special attendant, Yarfa’ to lash me with all his القوة as I bent to pick up the pearls. I started collecting the pearls and his attendant started lashing me. Then he told me. “O unfortunate man stand up!”
